intro-1.plaintext: To make your site editable with Hydra you load hydra.js in your frontend and configure it with your block types. Then you progressively add HTML annotations to enable visual editing features.

children.text: To make your site editable with Hydra you load hydra.js in your frontend and configure it with your block types. Then you progressively add HTML annotations to enable visual editing features.
Setting Up the Bridge

bridge-text.plaintext: Include hydra.js in your frontend and call initBridge() to create a two-way communication channel between Hydra and your frontend.

children.text: Include hydra.js in your frontend and call
initBridge()
to create a two-way communication channel between Hydra and your frontend. The bridge handles authentication, page navigation syncing, and block data transfer.
The
page.schema.properties
object configures which blocks fields your page has. The default field is
blocks_layout
. Each entry specifies a display title and which block types are allowed.
Multiple Page Blocks Fields

multi-text.plaintext: Pages can have multiple blocks fields for different areas like header, content and footer.

children.text: Pages can have multiple blocks fields for different areas (e.g., header, content, footer). Each shows as a separate section in the sidebar when no block is selected:
Path Transformation (pathToApiPath)

pathtransform-text.plaintext: If your frontend embeds state in the URL path (like pagination), use pathToApiPath to transform frontend paths to API paths.

children.text: If your frontend embeds state in the URL path (like pagination), you need to tell hydra.js how to transform the frontend path to the API/admin path. Otherwise, the admin will try to navigate to URLs that don't exist in the CMS.
The
pathToApiPath

function is called whenever hydra.js sends a PATH_CHANGE message to the admin, allowing your frontend to strip or transform URL segments that are frontend-specific (like pagination, filters, or other client-side state).
The Update Callback

callback-text.plaintext: Register onEditChange to receive live content updates as the user edits. Your frontend re-renders in realtime using the same data format as the Plone REST API.

children.text: Register
onEditChange
to receive live content updates as the user edits. Your frontend re-renders in realtime using the same data format as the Plone REST API.
Since the data structure is the same as returned by the Plone REST API, it's normally easy to re-render your page dynamically using the same code your frontend used to render the page initially.
With onEditChange registered, editors get:
Changes appear on the frontend as they type in the sidebar
Adding and removing blocks shows immediately in the preview
Drag and drop block reordering with live preview
A '+' icon on the frontend to add blocks after the current block
Note: For live updates you need a frontend deployed as SPA or hybrid rather than SSR or SSG. If your production deploy is SSG/SSR, deploy another edit-only preview frontend in SPA mode.
Custom Blocks

custom-text.plaintext: Define custom block types directly in your frontend configuration via the blocks option. No Volto plugin required.

children.text: Define custom block types directly in your frontend configuration via the
blocks
option in initBridge. No Volto plugin deployment required. Each block type needs an id, title, and a blockSchema with its field properties.
Child block types (like 'slide' above) must be defined at the top level of blocks. You can also:
Set
restricted: true
to hide a block from the block chooser (only usable as child blocks)
Set
mostUsed: true
to pin a block to the top of the chooser
Set
sidebarSchemaOnly: true
to use only the schema form in the sidebar (no custom edit component)
Use
fieldsets
in the schema to organize fields into tabs
Schema Enhancers

enhancers-text.plaintext: Schema enhancers modify block schemas dynamically. Use skiplogic for conditional field visibility.

children.text: Schema enhancers modify block schemas dynamically:
skiplogic
: Conditionally show/hide fields based on other field values.
field
: Field to check (use ../field for parent/page fields)
Operators: is, isNot, isSet, isNotSet, gt, gte, lt, lte
Block Conversion & fieldMappings

conversion-text.plaintext: Define fieldMappings to enable a Convert to... UI action between block types.

children.text: If a block type has a
fieldMappings
defined it will enable a "Convert to..." UI action. You specify either conversions to a specific type, or to a generic search result schema (@id, title, preview-image, description):

Transitive conversions are performed automatically by using paths through intermediate types (e.g., hero -> teaser -> image). Any fields that don't match will still be kept in the data so if the block is converted back that data will reappear.

inherit-heading.plaintext: Synchronised Block Types in a Container

children.text: Synchronised Block Types in a Container

inherit-text.plaintext: Use inheritSchemaFrom and childBlockConfig to synchronise child block types in a container.

children.text: You might want one container type that holds different block types but constrains them to all be the same type with synchronised settings. A field on the parent lets the editor select the type and all blocks get converted using fieldMappings:
inheritSchemaFrom
: Parent inherits schema from selected child type. When the type field changes, child blocks sync to new type.
typeField
: Field name for selecting child type (e.g., 'variation')
defaultsField
: Field name for storing inherited defaults (e.g., 'itemDefaults')
blocksField
: Which blocks field the sub-blocks are in. Set to ".." to use the parent's allowedBlocks.
filterConvertibleFrom
: Only allow selecting a block type which can convert from the specified type.
childBlockConfig
: Child hides fields except editableFields when inside a parent with inheritSchemaFrom.
HTML Annotations for Visual Editing

annotations-text.plaintext: Add data attributes to your rendered HTML to enable progressively richer visual editing.

children.text: Add data attributes to your rendered HTML to enable progressively richer visual editing:
data-block-uid="blockId"
— Click-to-select blocks. Hydra.js adds click handlers and shows a blue outline and Quanta toolbar on selected blocks.
data-edit-text="fieldName"
— Inline text editing. For simple text, click and type directly. For rich text (slate widget), select text to apply formatting via the Quanta toolbar.
data-edit-media="fieldName"
— Visual media uploading. Editors can upload, pick or drag-and-drop images directly onto the element.
data-edit-link="fieldName"
— Link editing. Click behaviour is replaced with a link picker to select content, enter an external URL, or open the link.
Example of a fully annotated slide block:

Big News

Check out hydra, it will change everything
Read more
Comment Syntax

comment-text.plaintext: If you can't modify the markup, use HTML comment syntax to specify block attributes.

children.text: If you can't modify the markup (e.g., using a 3rd party component library), use comment syntax to specify block attributes:

Title

Read more
Attributes without selectors apply to the root element: block-uid=xxx
Attributes with selectors target child elements: edit-text=title(.card-title)
Closing <!-- /hydra --> marks end of scope
Self-closing <!-- hydra block-uid=xxx /--> applies only to next sibling element
Supported attributes: block-uid, block-readonly, edit-text, edit-link, edit-media, block-add
Allowed Navigation (data-linkable-allow)

linkable-text.plaintext: Add data-linkable-allow to elements that should navigate during edit mode.

children.text: Add
data-linkable-allow
to elements that should navigate during edit mode (paging links, facet controls, etc.):
Next
Path Syntax for Parent/Page Fields

pathsyntax-text.plaintext: Use Unix-style paths in data-edit-* attributes to edit fields outside the current block.

children.text: The data-edit-text|edit-media|edit-link attributes support Unix-style paths to edit fields outside the current block:
fieldName
— edit the block's own field (default)
../fieldName
— edit the parent block's field
../../fieldName
— edit the grandparent's field
/fieldName
— edit the page metadata field

My Page Title

Page description here

Column Title
This allows fixed parts of the page (like headers) to be editable without being inside a block.
Readonly Regions

readonly-text.plaintext: Add data-block-readonly to disable inline editing for all fields inside an element.

children.text: Add
data-block-readonly
(or <!-- hydra block-readonly --> comment) to disable inline editing for all fields inside an element:

Target Page Title

Read more
Or using comment syntax:
...
Renderer Node-ID Rules

nodeid-text.plaintext: When rendering Slate nodes to DOM, your renderer must follow specific rules for data-node-id attributes.

children.text: When rendering Slate nodes to DOM, your renderer must follow these rules for
data-node-id
Element nodes (p, strong, em, etc.) must have data-node-id matching the Slate node's nodeId
Wrapper elements — If you add extra wrapper elements around a Slate node, ALL wrappers must have the same data-node-id as the inner element
hydra.js uses node-ids to map between Slate's data model and your DOM. When restoring cursor position after formatting changes, it walks your DOM counting Slate children.

Valid wrapper pattern: bold Both elements have the same node-id, so they count as one Slate child. Invalid (missing node-id on wrapper): bold This breaks cursor positioning because hydra.js can't correlate DOM structure to Slate structure.
Complete Slate Rendering Example
